ops.title: Update cc-skills ccx fragment entry 13 for ship fetch-first + auto-rebase
ops.description:
`ccx vcs ship` (jj lane) gained fetch-first + auto-rebase-when-safe (cc-context feature landing 2026-07-15): ship runs `jj git fetch` before bookmark resolution and, when the target bookmark (trunk or `--bookmark`) is no longer an ancestor of `@-`, rebases the stack onto it; a rebase that would conflict (or a conflicted/multi-head target bookmark) is rolled back / refused loudly.

Entry 13 of the shared ccx guide (cc-skills `plugin/guides/md/`, the `cc-skills:ccx` fragment consumed by every repo's AGENTS.md) still describes only the old behavior. After the next ccx release ships the feature (so the guide never describes behavior the installed brew binary lacks), extend entry 13's parenthetical with: "ship fetches first and, when the remote has moved past your base, rebases the stack onto the target bookmark before pushing — a rebase that would conflict is rolled back and reported instead of pushed."

Pipeline per cc-guides memory: edit the fragment in cc-skills, push, then bump the self-source lock pin (separate commit) and let consumers re-render. The cc-context-local Version-control fragment (`.claude/fragments/AGENTS.md/go-style.fragment.md`) already carries the equivalent wording — keep them consistent.

ops.text: cc-skills ccx fragment entry 13 mentions fetch-first + auto-rebase + conflict rollback
edit lands only after a ccx release containing shipPushJJ auto-rebase is published to the tap
consumer repos re-render cleanly (lock pin bumped)
